playbooks.name: Extract Function
playbooks.applies_when: A cohesive code block can be named by intent and called from its current owner.
preconditions: Behavior is covered by focused tests or a small characterization test.
Inputs, outputs, and side effects of the block are explicit.
steps: Extract the smallest cohesive block into a named function.
Replace the original block with a call.
Run focused tests and inspect call-site readability.
test_focus: Return values, side effects, and branch behavior around the extracted block.
risks: Capturing too many locals or changing evaluation order.

playbooks.name: Inline Function
playbooks.applies_when: A function adds indirection without clarifying behavior or protecting a boundary.
preconditions: All callers are known or the function is private/internal.
The function body is simple enough to inline safely.
steps: Inline one caller first or all private callers when trivial.
Remove the original function after callers are updated.
Run tests around former callers.
test_focus: Caller behavior and public API compatibility.
risks: Removing a useful semantic boundary or public extension point.

playbooks.name: Extract Variable
playbooks.applies_when: A complex expression needs an intention-revealing name.
preconditions: Expression evaluation has no hidden side effects.
Variable scope can stay narrow.
steps: Introduce a well-named local for the expression.
Replace the expression at the narrowest useful scope.
Run tests around the containing function.
test_focus: Evaluation order and expression result.
risks: Changing lazy evaluation or widening mutable state.

playbooks.name: Inline Variable
playbooks.applies_when: A variable obscures rather than clarifies a simple expression.
preconditions: The initializer is simple and side-effect free.
The variable is not part of debugging or public API behavior.
steps: Replace one use with the initializer expression.
Remove the variable after all uses are replaced.
Run tests around the containing function.
test_focus: Expression behavior and evaluation count.
risks: Duplicating expensive or side-effecting expressions.

playbooks.name: Change Function Declaration
playbooks.applies_when: A function name, parameter list, or signature no longer matches its role.
preconditions: Callers are known or a compatibility wrapper can be kept.
Tests cover representative call sites.
steps: Introduce the new declaration while preserving old behavior.
Update callers in small batches.
Remove the old declaration when no callers remain.
test_focus: Caller compatibility and external API usage.
risks: Breaking public callers, reflection, routing, or serialization hooks.

playbooks.name: Encapsulate Variable
playbooks.applies_when: Direct reads or writes to a variable need a controlled access boundary.
preconditions: Readers and writers can be located.
Accessor behavior can preserve current semantics first.
steps: Introduce accessors around the variable.
Move direct readers and writers to the accessors.
Restrict direct access after tests pass.
test_focus: Read/write behavior and mutation side effects.
risks: Changing initialization order or bypassing existing invariants.

playbooks.name: Rename Variable
playbooks.applies_when: A local, parameter, or variable name hides its role.
preconditions: Use symbol-aware rename where available.
Check external bindings for non-local variables.
steps: Rename the symbol mechanically.
Review nearby code for improved readability.
Run tests for the touched scope.
test_focus: Compilation, references, and dynamic binding paths.
risks: Breaking string-based references or generated bindings.

playbooks.name: Introduce Parameter Object
playbooks.applies_when: Several parameters travel together as one domain concept.
preconditions: The parameters are repeatedly passed together.
A clear object name exists for the group.
steps: Create the parameter object and pass it alongside old parameters.
Move reads to the object one caller at a time.
Remove old parameters after callers migrate.
test_focus: Call-site behavior and object construction defaults.
risks: Creating a vague data bag instead of a domain concept.

playbooks.name: Combine Functions into Class
playbooks.applies_when: Several functions operate on the same data and should share an owner.
preconditions: The shared data group is stable.
The functions have a cohesive responsibility.
steps: Create the class around the shared data.
Move one function as a method.
Move remaining cohesive functions after tests pass.
test_focus: Function outputs before and after moving into the class.
risks: Creating a large procedural class without cohesion.

playbooks.name: Combine Functions into Transform
playbooks.applies_when: Several functions enrich or transform the same input data.
preconditions: The transformation output is explicit.
Callers can consume the transformed result without hidden mutation.
steps: Create a transform that returns the enriched data.
Move one calculation into the transform.
Replace caller-side repeated calculations gradually.
test_focus: Transformed data fields and unchanged source data behavior.
risks: Mixing mutation and transformation semantics.

playbooks.name: Split Phase
playbooks.applies_when: One flow naturally separates into sequential phases with a clear handoff.
preconditions: The phase boundary and intermediate data are identifiable.
Tests cover the end-to-end flow.
steps: Introduce an explicit intermediate value between phases.
Extract the first phase.
Extract the second phase and rerun tests.
test_focus: Intermediate data shape and end-to-end output.
risks: Choosing an artificial boundary that increases coupling.

playbooks.name: Encapsulate Record
playbooks.applies_when: A record or loose data structure needs controlled access or behavior.
preconditions: Field readers and writers are known.
The replacement object can preserve field values first.
steps: Wrap the record in an object with accessors.
Move callers to the wrapper.
Hide or remove direct record access.
test_focus: Field access, serialization, and construction behavior.
risks: Breaking callers that depend on raw data shape.

playbooks.name: Encapsulate Collection
playbooks.applies_when: A collection field exposes uncontrolled mutation.
preconditions: Mutation paths are known.
A read-only or controlled update API is possible.
steps: Return a protected view or copy for reads.
Introduce add/remove methods for intended updates.
Remove direct collection mutation by callers.
test_focus: Collection contents, ordering, and mutability expectations.
risks: Changing aliasing, identity, or mutation semantics.

playbooks.name: Replace Primitive with Object
playbooks.applies_when: A primitive value represents a domain concept with rules or behavior.
preconditions: The concept has a meaningful name and validation rules.
Construction and comparison behavior can be preserved.
steps: Create a small value object around the primitive.
Replace one field or parameter use.
Move validation or formatting into the object.
test_focus: Equality, validation, formatting, and serialization.
risks: Over-wrapping incidental values with no behavior.

playbooks.name: Replace Temp with Query
playbooks.applies_when: A temporary value can be recalculated by a side-effect-free query.
preconditions: The calculation is deterministic in the current context.
Repeated evaluation is acceptable or cached elsewhere.
steps: Extract the temp initializer into a query.
Replace temp reads with the query.
Remove the temp when tests pass.
test_focus: Calculation result and evaluation side effects.
risks: Duplicating expensive or stateful calculations.

playbooks.name: Extract Class
playbooks.applies_when: Fields and methods form a cohesive responsibility inside a larger owner.
preconditions: A field/method cluster can be named.
Current behavior is characterized by tests.
steps: Create the new class with the cohesive data.
Move one behavior that uses that data.
Redirect callers through the new owner in small steps.
test_focus: Behavior using moved fields and original class integration.
risks: Splitting data from behavior or creating chatty coupling.

playbooks.name: Inline Class
playbooks.applies_when: A class is too small or indirect to justify its existence.
preconditions: The class has no meaningful independent lifecycle.
Callers can use the target owner directly.
steps: Move fields and methods into the target owner.
Redirect callers to the target owner.
Remove the inlined class after tests pass.
test_focus: Callers, construction paths, and serialization shape.
risks: Removing a useful API boundary or future extension point.

playbooks.name: Hide Delegate
playbooks.applies_when: Clients know too much about a delegate chain.
preconditions: The owning object can expose an intention-revealing operation.
The delegate boundary is stable.
steps: Add one forwarding method on the owner for the common need.
Move one caller to the owner method.
Repeat for useful operations only.
test_focus: Caller behavior and delegation target behavior.
risks: Creating a Middle Man by forwarding too much.

playbooks.name: Remove Middle Man
playbooks.applies_when: A class mostly forwards calls and adds no useful policy.
preconditions: Forwarders are not intentional facade or compatibility API.
Callers can depend on the delegate safely.
steps: Redirect one caller to the delegate.
Remove the corresponding forwarder when unused.
Repeat while tests stay green.
test_focus: Caller behavior and public API compatibility.
risks: Exposing internals or breaking facade boundaries.

playbooks.name: Substitute Algorithm
playbooks.applies_when: A clearer algorithm can replace an existing implementation with the same behavior.
preconditions: Expected behavior is well covered by examples or tests.
The replacement algorithm handles edge cases.
steps: Add tests for representative and edge cases.
Replace the algorithm in one step.
Compare behavior and performance where relevant.
test_focus: Edge cases, ordering, and performance-sensitive inputs.
risks: Changing obscure edge behavior.

playbooks.name: Move Function
playbooks.applies_when: A function uses another owner data or responsibility more than its current owner.
preconditions: The target owner is clear.
Dependencies on the current owner are explicit.
steps: Extract the movable logic if needed.
Add the function to the target owner.
Leave a forwarding wrapper until callers move.
test_focus: Current callers and target owner behavior.
risks: Moving behavior away from required state or API boundaries.

playbooks.name: Move Field
playbooks.applies_when: A field is used more naturally by another owner.
preconditions: The target owner lifecycle matches the field.
All reads and writes can be found.
steps: Add the field to the target owner.
Synchronize or redirect one access path.
Remove the original field after callers migrate.
test_focus: Reads, writes, initialization, and persistence mapping.
risks: Breaking object identity, persistence, or initialization order.

playbooks.name: Move Statements into Function
playbooks.applies_when: Repeated or preparatory statements belong inside the function they support.
preconditions: The moved statements are always needed by the function.
Moving them preserves order and side effects.
steps: Move the statements into the function body.
Remove duplicate caller-side setup.
Run caller tests.
test_focus: Call ordering and side effects.
risks: Moving caller-specific setup into shared behavior.

playbooks.name: Move Statements to Callers
playbooks.applies_when: Statements inside a function are needed only by some callers.
preconditions: Caller-specific behavior can be separated.
All affected callers are known.
steps: Copy the caller-specific statements to one caller.
Adjust the function to remove that special case.
Update remaining callers deliberately.
test_focus: Each caller variant and shared function behavior.
risks: Duplicating logic that should remain shared.

playbooks.name: Replace Inline Code with Function Call
playbooks.applies_when: Inline code duplicates behavior already available in a function.
preconditions: The existing function has equivalent semantics.
Inputs map cleanly to function parameters.
steps: Replace one inline block with the function call.
Run tests for that path.
Remove now-unused inline helpers if any.
test_focus: Behavioral equivalence for replaced code.
risks: Assuming similar code is exactly equivalent.

playbooks.name: Slide Statements
playbooks.applies_when: Nearby statements must be grouped to reveal extraction or sequencing.
preconditions: Moving statements does not change data dependencies.
Tests cover the containing function.
steps: Move one independent statement near related statements.
Run tests or compile after each move.
Extract or simplify after grouping is clear.
test_focus: Evaluation order and variable lifetimes.
risks: Crossing dependency or side-effect boundaries.

playbooks.name: Split Loop
playbooks.applies_when: One loop performs multiple independent tasks.
preconditions: Loop tasks do not depend on each other's per-iteration side effects.
Collection traversal cost is acceptable.
steps: Copy the loop for one task.
Remove the copied task from the original loop.
Run tests and simplify each loop.
test_focus: Accumulated results and side effects for each task.
risks: Changing ordering when tasks are coupled.

playbooks.name: Replace Loop with Pipeline
playbooks.applies_when: A loop expresses map/filter/reduce/query behavior better as a pipeline.
preconditions: Iteration side effects are isolated.
The language pipeline preserves needed ordering.
steps: Identify the pipeline stages.
Replace the loop with the simplest equivalent pipeline.
Run tests for empty and multi-item inputs.
test_focus: Ordering, short-circuit behavior, and empty collections.
risks: Obscuring imperative side effects or performance.

playbooks.name: Remove Dead Code
playbooks.applies_when: Code has no production caller or effect.
preconditions: No public, reflective, generated, or configuration path uses it.
Tests or search confirm it is unused.
steps: Remove the smallest unused element.
Run compile and focused tests.
Remove now-unused dependents.
test_focus: Build, public entrypoints, and integration configuration.
risks: Deleting dynamically referenced code.

playbooks.name: Split Variable
playbooks.applies_when: One variable represents multiple meanings over time.
preconditions: Each meaning can be named separately.
Assignments are understood.
steps: Introduce a new variable for one meaning.
Replace reads for that meaning.
Repeat until each variable has one purpose.
test_focus: Assignment order and computed values.
risks: Changing lifetime or stale value behavior.

playbooks.name: Rename Field
playbooks.applies_when: A field name hides its role or no longer matches the model.
preconditions: Use symbol-aware rename where possible.
Check persistence, JSON, reflection, and external contracts.
steps: Rename the field mechanically.
Update explicit mappings if needed.
Run tests and serialization checks.
test_focus: Field access and external data binding.
risks: Breaking serialized names or database mapping.

playbooks.name: Replace Derived Variable with Query
playbooks.applies_when: A stored value can be derived from existing state.
preconditions: The derivation is deterministic and cheap enough.
Writers to the derived variable are known.
steps: Create a query that computes the value.
Replace reads with the query.
Remove writes and storage after tests pass.
test_focus: Derived value consistency after mutations.
risks: Changing caching or performance characteristics.

playbooks.name: Change Reference to Value
playbooks.applies_when: An object is best treated as an immutable value.
preconditions: Identity is not meaningful to callers.
Equality can be value-based.
steps: Make state immutable or replacement-based.
Implement value equality where needed.
Replace mutations with new values.
test_focus: Equality, copying, and update behavior.
risks: Breaking identity-based caches or shared mutation expectations.

playbooks.name: Change Value to Reference
playbooks.applies_when: Several equal values should refer to one shared identity.
preconditions: Identity matters in the domain.
A lookup or repository owner exists.
steps: Introduce a lookup for canonical instances.
Replace direct construction on one path.
Move remaining construction through the lookup.
test_focus: Identity equality, lifecycle, and caching.
risks: Introducing hidden global state or stale references.

playbooks.name: Decompose Conditional
playbooks.applies_when: A condition or branches need intention-revealing names.
preconditions: Each condition or branch can be named by intent.
Branch behavior is covered by tests.
steps: Extract the condition into a named query.
Extract complex then/else logic into named functions.
Run branch-focused tests.
test_focus: Boundary cases for each branch.
risks: Changing boolean logic while extracting.

playbooks.name: Consolidate Conditional Expression
playbooks.applies_when: Several checks lead to the same result and should express one concept.
preconditions: The checks have the same outcome and no order-dependent side effects.
A combined concept name exists.
steps: Combine equivalent checks into one expression or query.
Name the combined condition.
Run tests for each former path.
test_focus: All former condition paths.
risks: Changing short-circuit side effects.

playbooks.name: Replace Nested Conditional with Guard Clauses
playbooks.applies_when: Nested conditionals obscure the main path.
preconditions: Exceptional or edge cases can return early safely.
Resource cleanup behavior is preserved.
steps: Extract one edge case as a guard clause.
Flatten the remaining main path.
Run branch and cleanup tests.
test_focus: Early returns and main happy path.
risks: Skipping cleanup or finally-style behavior.

playbooks.name: Replace Conditional with Polymorphism
playbooks.applies_when: Repeated branches vary behavior by stable type or state.
preconditions: Variants are stable domain concepts.
Tests cover every branch.
steps: Extract branch behavior into named operations.
Introduce one variant type or strategy.
Move one branch at a time into variant behavior.
test_focus: Each variant and default/error behavior.
risks: Creating a class hierarchy for unstable branches.

playbooks.name: Introduce Special Case
playbooks.applies_when: Repeated null or exceptional value handling can be represented by an object.
preconditions: The special condition is well-defined.
Callers expect consistent behavior for it.
steps: Create the special-case object/value.
Replace one conditional check with polymorphic behavior.
Move remaining checks gradually.
test_focus: Special case and normal case behavior.
risks: Hiding real errors behind a special object.

playbooks.name: Introduce Assertion
playbooks.applies_when: An assumption should be made executable and checked.
preconditions: The condition is a true invariant, not normal validation.
Failures should be visible during development or runtime.
steps: Add the narrowest assertion near the assumption.
Run tests for valid paths.
Add/adjust tests for invalid assumptions when appropriate.
test_focus: Invariant boundaries and failure mode.
risks: Using assertions for user-input validation.

playbooks.name: Separate Query from Modifier
playbooks.applies_when: A function both returns data and changes state.
preconditions: The returned query result can be computed without mutation.
Mutation callers are identifiable.
steps: Extract a side-effect-free query.
Update callers that only need the value.
Keep mutation in a separate command.
test_focus: State changes and returned values independently.
risks: Changing timing of side effects.

playbooks.name: Parameterize Function
playbooks.applies_when: Similar functions differ only by values or small operations.
preconditions: The varying value has a clear parameter name.
Call sites stay readable with the parameter.
steps: Add the parameter to one function.
Redirect duplicate functions to the parameterized version.
Remove duplicates after tests pass.
test_focus: Each former function behavior.
risks: Adding flag-like parameters that obscure intent.

playbooks.name: Remove Flag Argument
playbooks.applies_when: A parameter switches between distinct behaviors.
preconditions: Each behavior can have an intention-revealing entrypoint.
Callers are known.
steps: Create explicit functions for each behavior.
Update callers to the explicit functions.
Remove the flag argument.
test_focus: Both flag paths and caller readability.
risks: Replacing one unclear API with several unclear names.

playbooks.name: Preserve Whole Object
playbooks.applies_when: Callers pass many values extracted from the same object.
preconditions: The callee should reasonably know about the source object.
Passing the whole object does not expose unrelated internals.
steps: Add an overload or path accepting the whole object.
Move one caller to pass the object.
Remove extracted parameters when callers migrate.
test_focus: Call-site behavior and object field usage.
risks: Increasing coupling to a broad object.

playbooks.name: Replace Parameter with Query
playbooks.applies_when: A parameter value can be obtained from another available object or query.
preconditions: The query is available at the callee without new hidden dependencies.
The query has no unwanted side effects.
steps: Add the query inside the function.
Stop passing the redundant parameter from one caller.
Remove the parameter after all callers migrate.
test_focus: Caller behavior and query result.
risks: Making dependencies less explicit.

playbooks.name: Replace Query with Parameter
playbooks.applies_when: A function should receive a value rather than reach outward for it.
preconditions: The caller can provide the value clearly.
Passing the value reduces hidden dependencies.
steps: Add the parameter while keeping the query fallback if needed.
Pass the value from one caller.
Remove internal query use after migration.
test_focus: Caller-provided values and dependency isolation.
risks: Pushing too much calculation to callers.

playbooks.name: Remove Setting Method
playbooks.applies_when: A field should not be changed after construction or controlled setup.
preconditions: All setter callers are known.
Construction or controlled update paths can supply the value.
steps: Move one setter use into construction or a controlled command.
Delete the setter when no callers remain.
Run mutation and construction tests.
test_focus: Initialization and update behavior.
risks: Blocking legitimate lifecycle transitions.

playbooks.name: Replace Constructor with Factory Function
playbooks.applies_when: Object creation needs naming, variants, caching, or preconditions.
preconditions: Factory naming clarifies construction intent.
Callers can be migrated safely.
steps: Add a factory that delegates to the constructor.
Move callers to the factory.
Restrict constructor visibility if appropriate.
test_focus: Construction variants and defaults.
risks: Hiding expensive or surprising creation logic.

playbooks.name: Replace Function with Command
playbooks.applies_when: A function is hard to decompose because it carries many locals or phases.
preconditions: Behavior is characterized by tests.
Command state can remain private to the operation.
steps: Create a command object with the function inputs.
Move the function body into an execute method.
Extract phases from command state.
test_focus: Original function behavior and command lifecycle.
risks: Creating mutable command state without simplifying behavior.

playbooks.name: Replace Command with Function
playbooks.applies_when: A command object no longer needs state or extensibility.
preconditions: The command has one simple operation.
Callers do not rely on command identity or lifecycle.
steps: Introduce an equivalent function.
Move callers to the function.
Remove the command class after tests pass.
test_focus: Callers and side effects of the command.
risks: Removing a useful extension or lifecycle boundary.

playbooks.name: Pull Up Method
playbooks.applies_when: Sibling subclasses share equivalent behavior.
preconditions: The method behavior is truly common.
Superclass dependencies are available or can be abstracted.
steps: Move the method to the superclass.
Adjust subclass-specific dependencies.
Remove duplicate subclass methods.
test_focus: All affected subclasses.
risks: Forcing uncommon behavior into the superclass.

playbooks.name: Pull Up Field
playbooks.applies_when: Sibling subclasses declare the same field for the same concept.
preconditions: The field has the same meaning and lifecycle in subclasses.
Initialization can be unified.
steps: Add the field to the superclass.
Redirect subclass access to the superclass field.
Remove duplicate subclass fields.
test_focus: Subclass initialization and field access.
risks: Merging similar-looking fields with different semantics.

playbooks.name: Pull Up Constructor Body
playbooks.applies_when: Subclass constructors repeat initialization that belongs in the superclass.
preconditions: Repeated constructor code is equivalent.
Superclass initialization order is safe.
steps: Move common initialization into the superclass constructor or helper.
Call it from subclasses.
Remove duplicate initialization.
test_focus: Construction order and subclass defaults.
risks: Changing initialization order or overridable calls.

playbooks.name: Push Down Method
playbooks.applies_when: A superclass method is only meaningful for some subclasses.
preconditions: Only specific subclasses need the method.
Callers can depend on the narrower type or polymorphic contract.
steps: Move the method to the subclass that needs it.
Update references and abstract contracts as needed.
Run hierarchy tests.
test_focus: Subclass behavior and superclass contract.
risks: Breaking callers typed as the superclass.

playbooks.name: Push Down Field
playbooks.applies_when: A superclass field is only meaningful for some subclasses.
preconditions: Only specific subclasses use the field.
Initialization can move with the field.
steps: Add the field to the relevant subclass.
Move initialization and access.
Remove the superclass field after tests pass.
test_focus: Subclass construction and field behavior.
risks: Breaking superclass-level behavior or serialization.

playbooks.name: Replace Type Code with Subclasses
playbooks.applies_when: A type code drives variant-specific behavior.
preconditions: Type code values represent stable variants.
Tests cover each variant.
steps: Encapsulate construction for the type code.
Introduce one subclass for one variant.
Move variant behavior out of conditionals.
test_focus: Variant behavior and factory selection.
risks: Creating subclasses for volatile or combinatorial states.

playbooks.name: Remove Subclass
playbooks.applies_when: A subclass adds no meaningful behavior or distinction.
preconditions: The subclass has no required separate lifecycle or API role.
Callers can use the superclass or another variant.
steps: Move needed behavior or data up or sideways.
Replace construction of the subclass.
Delete the subclass after callers migrate.
test_focus: Construction and type checks involving the subclass.
risks: Breaking type-based external contracts.

playbooks.name: Extract Superclass
playbooks.applies_when: Several classes share behavior or interface that deserves a common abstraction.
preconditions: The shared role is real and stable.
Common behavior can move without weakening class responsibilities.
steps: Create the superclass with one shared member.
Move shared behavior or fields gradually.
Update clients only when the abstraction helps.
test_focus: All subclasses and shared behavior.
risks: Creating speculative or leaky inheritance.

playbooks.name: Collapse Hierarchy
playbooks.applies_when: A superclass/subclass split no longer carries useful variation.
preconditions: Hierarchy levels have no meaningful separate responsibilities.
Callers do not depend on the distinction.
steps: Move members into the surviving class.
Update construction and type references.
Remove the empty hierarchy level.
test_focus: Type checks, construction, and inherited behavior.
risks: Removing a useful polymorphic extension point.

playbooks.name: Replace Subclass with Delegate
playbooks.applies_when: Subclass variation is better represented by composition.
preconditions: Inheritance causes coupling or rejected behavior.
Delegated behavior can preserve the public API first.
steps: Add a delegate field for one variant behavior.
Forward one behavior through the delegate.
Replace subclass-specific behavior gradually.
test_focus: Subclass public behavior and delegation paths.
risks: Duplicating behavior during migration.

playbooks.name: Replace Superclass with Delegate
playbooks.applies_when: A superclass relationship exposes too much or models the wrong relationship.
preconditions: The class should use, not be, the superclass concept.
Delegation can preserve required behavior.
steps: Add a delegate to the former superclass behavior.
Forward required methods through the delegate.
Remove the inheritance link after callers are safe.
test_focus: Inherited behavior, public API, and construction.
risks: Breaking substitutability or superclass contract assumptions.
